summary: yandex-geobase performs install-time beacon to attacker-controlled bare IP, dependency-confusion shape against Yandex internal namespace
details: Package squats the name `yandex-geobase`, consistent with an internal Yandex namespace. The `postinstall` script issues an HTTP GET to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint (`http://130.49.177.51:18080/p/dc-20260627-yandex-geobase`) transmitting the package name, version, and a fixed dated nonce. This is the canonical dependency-confusion install-confirmation telemetry pattern: when a Yandex-internal build misresolves to this public package, the attacker is notified that the misresolution succeeded. The beacon fires automatically on `npm install` via the postinstall lifecycle hook, with no opt-in or disclosure. While the current beacon body does not exfiltrate installer secrets, the package's only purpose is to confirm dependency-confusion success against a specific target organization — it should not be installed by anyone other than the operator running it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
